how_it_works: Unique flasks have fixed notable effects beyond what magic flasks can roll. They still accept a prefix (Surgeon's, Seething, Chemist's, Catalysed) and a suffix (of Staunching, of Heat, of Warding, etc.). Combining a strong unique effect with the right craft is part of flask setup optimisation.
craft_note: Surgeon's prefix (20% chance to gain a charge on critical strike) enables near-permanent uptime on crit builds. Seething prefix grants instant partial recovery on use.
tips: Most builds use 1-2 unique flasks for damage/defence and fill the rest with magic utility flasks
The Wise Oak: balance all uncapped resistances equally to receive both penetration (for you) and resistance reduction (for monsters) on all elements
Progenesis delays damage — activate BEFORE taking a big hit, not after, for maximum value
Surgeon's prefix on Atziri's Promise or Vessel of Vinktar enables uptime on most crit builds
Dying Sun's AoE bonus is multiplicative with other AoE increases — very strong on builds already stacking AoE

flasks.name: Atziri's Promise
flasks.effect: 2% of Chaos Damage Leeched as Life. Gain 15% of Elemental Damage as Extra Chaos Damage during Effect.
flasks.when_to_use: Activate alongside main damage window — the leech provides minor sustain, the chaos bonus scales with elemental hits
flasks.notes: Extremely accessible and broadly useful. One of the most commonly used DPS flasks in the game.

flasks.name: The Wise Oak
flasks.effect: For each uncapped Elemental Resistance that is equal: Penetrates 15% of that Resistance / Enemies take 8% increased Damage of that type.
flasks.when_to_use: Active as long as you have charges — aim for full uptime
flasks.notes: Max value when all three elements are at the same uncapped value (e.g. all at 75%). Gives both offensive penetration and a monster debuff.

flasks.name: Dying Sun
flasks.effect: +2 to Projectiles during Effect. 20% increased Area of Effect during Effect.
flasks.when_to_use: Activate before engaging large packs or bosses with projectile skills
flasks.notes: Strong on any projectile skill (Arc, Ball Lightning, Rain of Arrows). AoE bonus also benefits melee AoE skills.

flasks.name: Bottled Faith
flasks.effect: Creates Consecrated Ground on Use. Consecrated Ground from this Flask has Tripled Radius. Consecrated Ground from this Flask grants 2% Life Regeneration.
flasks.when_to_use: Stand in the consecrated ground ring during boss fights — regeneration and damage bonus are significant
flasks.notes: Consecrated ground grants +50% damage to enemies standing in it. Extremely valuable on most endgame builds. Expensive but widely used.

flasks.name: Cinderswallow Urn
flasks.effect: Recover 1% of Life per Enemy Ignited by you during Effect. Nearby enemies take 8% increased Damage while you are Igniting at least one enemy.
flasks.when_to_use: Keep active during mapping — the regen and damage bonus are passive while you ignite enemies
flasks.notes: Best on builds that reliably ignite. Magnified Flask suffix can add a powerful extra modifier. Great synergy with Elementalist.

flasks.name: Vessel of Vinktar
flasks.effect: Adds 1 to 40 Lightning Damage to Attacks during Effect (variants available for Spells, Attacks, or Attack Leeches Mana instead of Life).
flasks.when_to_use: Activate for boss fights or dense packs — the added damage scales well with multipliers
flasks.notes: Four variants exist (Attacks, Spells, Penetration, Mana). Pick the one matching your skill type. Penetration variant is most universally valued.

flasks.name: Witchfire Brew
flasks.effect: Grants Level 21 Despair Curse Aura during Effect.
flasks.when_to_use: Active during all boss fights — Despair reduces chaos resistance
flasks.notes: Free Despair curse without needing to socket a curse gem. Excellent budget option for chaos builds that can't self-curse otherwise.

flasks.name: Taste of Hate
flasks.effect: 30% of Physical Damage taken as Cold. 20% chance to Avoid being Chilled during Effect. 20% chance to Avoid being Frozen during Effect.
flasks.when_to_use: Always active in endgame — converts a portion of physical hits to manageable cold damage
flasks.notes: One of the best physical damage mitigation tools in the game. Stacks with other phys-to-X conversion. Particularly strong on high-evasion builds where physical hits bypass your evasion.

flasks.name: Rumi's Concoction
flasks.effect: +3000 to Armour during Effect. 25% Chance to Block Attack Damage. 12% Chance to Block Spell Damage.
flasks.when_to_use: Active during boss fights or dangerous packs for the block layers
flasks.notes: Provides both armour and a large flat block bonus. Essential for block-based builds. Pairs well with Rainbowstride or other block sources.

flasks.name: Lion's Roar
flasks.effect: +3000 to Armour during Effect. Knocks Back Enemies on Hit during Effect. 20% More Melee Physical Damage during Effect.
flasks.when_to_use: Active during boss fights for the More multiplier — knockback disrupts some boss mechanics
flasks.notes: The 'More Melee Physical' suffix makes this a strong DPS flask for melee builds. Knockback can interrupt some boss abilities — valuable or annoying depending on context.

flasks.name: Sin's Rebirth
flasks.effect: Gain Unholy Might during Effect. Creates a Smoke Cloud on Use. 10% chance to Blind Enemies on Hit during Effect.
flasks.when_to_use: Activate before engaging — smoke cloud creates a blind zone
flasks.notes: Unholy Might adds 30% of Physical Damage as Extra Chaos Damage. Blind on hit is valuable for evasion builds. The smoke cloud blocks enemy projectiles.

flasks.name: Olroth's Resolve
flasks.effect: Grants Fortify during Effect. You are not slowed to below base movement speed during Effect. Fortification stacks above maximum Fortification during Effect.
best_builds: Marauder/Duelist builds that want Fortify uptime
flasks.when_to_use: Active during boss fights for the Fortify bonus — especially valuable if your build has no other Fortify source
flasks.notes: Gives Fortify without needing a melee hit. Excellent for bow or spell builds that want the 20% less damage taken from Fortify. No movement penalty is a QoL bonus.

flasks.name: Forbidden Taste
flasks.effect: Instant 100% Life Recovery. Lose 50% of your Life after Recovery over 4 seconds.
best_builds: Builds with good life regen to offset the penalty
flasks.when_to_use: Emergency only — activate when about to die, NOT during normal combat
flasks.notes: Full instant recovery is unmatched for emergencies but the 50% life loss over 4 seconds requires strong regen or instant resumption of combat to offset. Do not use proactively.

flasks.name: Progenesis
flasks.effect: 25% of Damage from Hits is taken from the Flask's remaining Charges first. Movement Speed bonus is not removed at Full Charges.
flasks.when_to_use: Pre-emptively before a dangerous boss hit — it only absorbs damage if charges remain
flasks.notes: Revolutionised HC-adjacent play. The charge consumption acts as a second life buffer. Combine with charge-recovery mechanics (Surgeon's, Pathfinder) for sustained value.

flasks.name: Coruscating Elixir
flasks.effect: During Effect, take no Chaos Damage. During Effect, all Chaos Damage you would take bypasses Energy Shield instead targeting your Life.
flasks.when_to_use: Active during boss fights with chaos damage (Sirus, Shaper storms, Blight, Ritual)
flasks.notes: Effectively grants chaos immunity during the flask window. Requires having life available (useless on pure CI). Very strong counter to Sirus storm damage.

flasks.name: Rotgut
flasks.effect: Gain a Frenzy Charge on Use. Gain Onslaught for 4 seconds on Using a Flask. Gain Onslaught for 4 seconds when you Kill an Enemy.
flasks.when_to_use: Active during mapping for the frenzy charge and Onslaught combo
flasks.notes: Provides both a Frenzy charge and Onslaught without needing to kill — useful for bossing where frenzy uptime is otherwise hard to maintain.

flasks.name: Divination Distillate
flasks.effect: 20% increased Rarity of Items found. 5% increased Quantity of Items found. Magic and Rare Monsters found have 3 additional drops during Effect.
flasks.when_to_use: Active while killing rare and unique monsters — the 'additional drops' effect scales with magic/rare count
flasks.notes: Very useful for dedicated magic find builds. Most valuable in high-monster-density maps with Nemesis-style mods.

flasks.name: Atziri's Foible
flasks.effect: 100% increased Mana Regeneration Rate. Regenerate 3% of Life per second during Effect. Item Attribute Requirements are 100% reduced.
flasks.when_to_use: Used primarily for the attribute requirement reduction — swap for gear that would otherwise require more Str/Dex/Int
flasks.notes: The attribute reduction allows equipping items 100% above your base attributes. Mainly a budget/flexibility tool rather than endgame power option.
